  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Concrete segregation observed due to excessive drop height during placement",
    "Violation of maximum free-fall distance limits"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt the concrete placement immediately",
    "Reject the segregated concrete already placed in the affected area",
    "Implement drop chutes, trunks, or tremies to limit free-fall distance to under 5 feet (1.5 meters)",
    "Re-verify mix consistency and consolidation procedures before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "Placing concrete from an excessive height causes the coarse aggregate to separate from the mortar (segregation), leading to honeycombing, structural voids, and compromised compressive strength. Under ACI 301 and 304, placement must be stopped immediately to correct the deposition method using proper equipment like drop chutes.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 301 Specifications for Structural Concrete",
    "ACI 304R Guide for Measuring, Mixing, Transporting, and Placing Concrete"
  ]
}
